results.title: iOSWorld: A Benchmark for Personally Intelligent Phone Agents - arXiv
results.content: iOSWorld is the first dynamic native iOS simulator benchmark built around a user’s personal identity. We built 26 native iOS applications and populated them with connected data for a single persona, Jordan Avery. The same contacts appear across messaging, payment, and email. A food order on one app produces a bank charge and a receipt email in others. An upcoming flight matches a hotel booking and confirmation emails across separate apps. We release 133 tasks in three categories. Single-app tasks (27) test basic interaction within one app. Multi-app tasks (60) carry information across 2 to 8 applications. Memory and personalization tasks (46) require agents to discover implicit patterns from in-app data without being told where to look. The release includes a schema for adding new tasks [...] A useful phone agent needs to be personally intelligent. It should reason over a user’s identity, history, and preferences as they exist on the device, not just follow isolated instructions in an impersonal sandbox. Existing mobile agent benchmarks lack this kind of personalization. We introduce iOSWorld, the first interactive native iOS simulator benchmark built around a persistent user identity spanning 26 newly built iOS apps. These apps contain connected data such as transactions, messages, travel records, social relationships, and financial activity. iOSWorld includes 133 tasks across three increasingly difficult categories. Single-app tasks (27) test one app, multi-app tasks (60) span 2 to 8 apps, and memory and personalization tasks (46) require agents to infer patterns from [...] iOSWorld is an interactive native iOS benchmark with a persistent user identity across 26 apps. The best vision+XML configuration reaches 93% on single-app tasks, but only 37% on multi-app and 54% on memory tasks. Qwen3.5 35B-A3B trails at 11% overall. Even frontier models often run out of room, with 51% of their failures exhausting the 50-step budget. Closing this gap will require stronger loop recovery, better action and visual grounding, and planning that is aware of the user’s history. We release the code, environments, and an AWS runner at . Our environment and open-source code allow seamless addition of new tasks, personas, and apps. We believe that iOSWorld can provide a strong foundation for furthering the research on mobile agents and a shift towards emphasizing the

results.title: 2025 iOS and Android Alternatives - Gadget Salvation Blog
results.content:
### Conclusion

The world beyond iOS and Android is rich with diverse mobile operating systems, each offering a unique set of features, philosophies, and trade-offs. Whether you prioritize privacy, customization, open-source principles, or simply seek a different mobile experience, the alternatives available in 2025 provide compelling options to explore. As the mobile landscape continues to evolve, these alternatives play a crucial role in fostering competition, innovation, and user choice in the way we connect with the digital world. And finally, if you want to switch things up and try a different software or a mobile brand altogether, Gadget Salvation is a buyback platform where you can sell your smartphone and use that extra cash to purchase something different and exciting. [...] ### Emerging and Niche Options:

 Sailfish OS (by Jolla): A Linux-based mobile operating system with a unique gesture-based user interface and a focus on privacy. It offers some compatibility with Android apps through an emulator.
 PureOS (by Purism): A fully free and open-source Linux distribution with a strong focus on security and privacy, also powering Purism’s Librem 5 smartphone.

## Choosing the Right Alternative

Selecting an alternative to iOS or Android depends heavily on individual needs and priorities. [...] ## The Landscape of iOS and Android Alternatives in 2025

The mobile OS landscape beyond iOS and Android is diverse and constantly evolving. Here are some of the most notable alternatives available in 2025:

### Privacy and Security Focused:

results.title: 15 Best Alternative App Stores for Android & iPhone in 2026
results.content:
### 15. AltStore (Global)

Outside the European Union, the original version of AltStore remains available to iOS users worldwide. The setup requires a Mac or Windows computer running AltServer, which signs and refreshes app certificates using the user's personal Apple Developer account. While this extra step limits mainstream adoption, it has not prevented AltStore from building a committed global following.

AltStore became widely known in 2024 when Apple updated its policies to allow emulators on iOS. Delta, the Nintendo emulator built by AltStore's own team, became one of the most talked-about iOS apps of 2024 and introduced millions of users to the idea of sideloading for the first time.

Key details for developers: [...] Regulatory changes in Europe, rapid device growth in Asia, and a growing community of privacy-focused users have created a healthy ecosystem of alternative app stores. In 2026, these platforms are not fallback options. For many developers and businesses, they are primary distribution channels.

According to recent Statista tracking data, Google Play remains the world's largest app marketplace with well over 2 million apps available, while the Apple App Store continues to host more than 1.5 million apps globally. However, raw app counts only tell part of the story. In China, Google Play is blocked entirely. In India and Southeast Asia, Xiaomi and Huawei devices ship with their own stores already installed. In Europe, the regulatory door to iOS alternatives is now wide open. [...] The platform is particularly useful for accessing region-locked games from Japan, China, and South Korea that are not officially released in Western markets, and it does this without requiring a VPN or a secondary app store account.

Key details for developers:

If your game targets anime fans, gacha game players, or users who follow Japanese and Korean gaming culture, QooApp reaches that audience more effectively than any general-purpose store can.

## iOS Alternative App Stores

The following platforms are specifically for iOS distribution. Most are currently available to EU users, where Apple is required to permit third-party stores under the Digital Markets Act. Wider global availability is expected as similar regulations develop in other regions.

### 11. AltStore PAL

# Please help me find iOS app alternatives to my Android apps : r/ios

Open menu Open navigation?

6.   NewPipe or Vanced for ad-free YouTube browsing and ability to download videos on wifi and watch them later.

7.   Screen Filter is an app that can lower the screen brightness way below what the system normally permits users to.

8.   Any app that shows me all my missed or dismissed notifications

9.   Any app that enables me to split my bluetooth output. For example, Youtube should play on the bluetooth headphone and a game on the iPhone speaker?

Thanks in advance!

Built to scale with you from the start. Codex, available with ChatGPT.

Sign Up [...] ### Leveraging Built-in iOS Features

   Apple Shortcuts allows users to create custom, multi-step workflows to automate repetitive tasks, significantly enhancing productivity. "I don’t think a single app has enhanced my productivity anywhere close to Shortcuts."

   Apple Notes, Reminders, and Calendar are often sufficient for basic productivity needs, offering strong synchronization across Apple devices. "Honestly just the default notes, calendar and reminders app."

   Text replacement shortcuts and gestures like long-tapping the space bar to move the cursor are underrated features that can save time. "People usually have their minds blown when I show them you can long tap the space bar to move your cursor when typing, no one seems to know this." [...] ### Niche and Specialized Productivity Tools

   Forest uses emotional blackmail to encourage focus by growing a digital tree when you stay off distracting apps. "Forest: Keeps me off TikTok using pure emotional blackmail."

   SinceWhen is a minimalist app that tracks when you last did something, helping build a timeline for habits or events. "The App SinceWhen is a minimalist iOS app that tracks when you last did something."

Are you interested in exploring apps for a specific type of productivity, such as habit tracking or project management?
